title: Chapter 1: The Awakening
description: Nor Verner discovers a strange mana source beneath the academy grounds.
excerpt: A dormant power stirs in the foundations of the Miron Academy, changing everything for Nor.

content.text: The academy walls breathed ancient cold as the first light of dawn slid between the towers. Nor felt the familiar ache behind their ribs — a hunger that had arrived with the strange pulse beneath the training courtyard.

It was not the academy's lessons that called him. It was something older, hidden in the stone and shadows. When his hand brushed the mossy slab near the east gate, energy flared across his skin; the air shivered, and for a breath, the world held its breath with him.

content.caption: The hidden pulse of mana beneath the academy.

content.text: A voice, not a voice, whispered with the same cadence as his heartbeat. It promised power and clarity, creation and destruction intertwined. Nor recoiled, but the sensation left a mark on his soul, an invitation he could not simply ignore.

That evening, in the lantern-lit hall, Orlan's laughter rang hollow. He saw Nor's eyes and knew something had shifted. The academy had taught them how to control mana, but nothing had prepared them for a force that answered the user as much as the user answered it.

title: Chapter 2: The First Duel
description: A challenge forces Nor to confront the power that is changing him.
excerpt: In the arena of the academy, friends become rivals and the first duel exposes a hidden strength.

content.text: The training arena hummed with the energy of a hundred waiting eyes. Nor stepped into the ring, pulse steady, as Edora watched from the front row. The crowd expected spectacle; they would see something more dangerous.

His opponent was a veteran of the academy's trial matches, a student whose mastery of wind mana made him unpredictable. Yet Nor's hands did not tremble. The pulse under his skin burned, steady and fierce, as if the ground itself supported him.

As the duel began, Nor did not cast a spell. He reached inward, coaxing the strange current that had spoken to him. Light flared, not the raw flash of established mana, but a braided glow — creation twisting around destruction. The arena fell silent.

When the fight ended, the opponent lay on the mat, breathless and humbled. The academy's masters exchanged glances. Nor's power had surfaced in ways no textbooks covered, and the path ahead had just become far more perilous.

title: Chapter 3: The Scholar's Warning
description: Orlan shares a secret from the archive and the first threat to Nor's secret appears.
excerpt: A late-night discovery in the archives reveals that Nor is not the only one touched by the ancient force.

content.text: The stone archive smelled of parchment and cold incense. Orlan moved through the stacks with quiet purpose, carrying a lantern that painted the shelves in gold. He did not look like a lone scholar — he looked like someone holding a truth too heavy for daylight.

Nor read the old ledger with fingers that shook. An entry mentioned a mana source from the age before the Miron Empire was forged. It spoke of a being who could weave creation and destruction from a single breath.

content.caption: A secret passage of the academy archive.

content.text: The warning came not from the page, but from the shadow at the end of the hall. A master of the academy emerged, eyes narrowed. He knew the meaning of the ledger, and he knew what it meant if Nor's power was the same ancient current.

That night, the academy was no longer just a school. It was a battleground of ideas, loyalties, and hidden histories. Nor understood the price of the awakening: the world would be forced to decide whether his power was salvation or damnation.

title: Chapter 4: The Emperor's Eye
description: The Miron Empire sends a watcher to the academy and political tension rises.
excerpt: At the heart of the empire, new eyes are cast upon the academy as Nor's strange mana begins to ripple outward.

content.text: A crimson banner hung above the imperial palace, fluttering with a reminder that the Miron Empire saw all. A courier arrived at the academy with a sealed letter, the mark of the emperor stamped in iron.
Nor sensed the change before anyone spoke. The air around the palace had shifted, thicker and sharper. The empire was no longer content to let the academy shape warriors in private.

content.caption: The imperial envoy arrives at the academy.

content.text: The letter demanded an inspection and a report. A special envoy, skilled in politics and secrecy, would arrive within the week. Nor realized that his power had become more than a personal secret — it was a question the emperor wanted answered.

Some would call it a summons; others would call it a threat. Either way, the academy's halls would teem with whispering courtiers, and the next chapter of Nor's journey would be written under the empire's eye.
